title: rhododendron retusum var. retusum
intro: Rhododendron retusum var. retusum, also known as the 'Mume' rhododendron, is a deciduous shrub native to East Asia, characterized by its unique, leathery leaves and clusters of white, pink, or purple flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which can help to treat various health issues, including fever, sore throats, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rhododendron retusum var. retusum include its ability to boost the immune system, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit antimicrobial and anti-viral properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or infusion, and its flowers and leaves are used to make tinctures, extracts, and dried preparations for medicinal purposes.
